metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Computer Studies 10 provides comprehensive instruction in computer systems, programming fundamentals, and digital problem-solving. Students learn how computers work at a foundational level, write code in multiple languages, and apply computational thinking to solve real-world problems. They explore cybersecurity, data structures, and the societal impact of computing while developing practical skills in software development.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on computing fundamentals, programming, and computational thinking.

big_ideas.statement: Computational thinking—breaking problems into parts, recognizing patterns, and abstracting solutions—is a powerful approach to problem-solving.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn that computational thinking is useful far beyond programming. By decomposing problems, identifying patterns, and creating general solutions, they can tackle complex challenges across many domains.

big_ideas.statement: Programming is a craft that combines logic, creativity, and precision to bring ideas to life.
big_ideas.elaboration: Writing code requires careful thinking, testing, and iteration. Students discover that coding is both an art and a science—it requires both logical reasoning and creative problem-solving.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding how computers work enables more thoughtful and ethical use of technology.
big_ideas.elaboration: By learning how hardware and software interact, how data is stored and processed, and how security works, students become more informed technology users and citizens.

big_ideas.statement: Computing shapes society in powerful ways that raise important questions about access, privacy, bias, and equity.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore how computing systems affect different communities, how bias can be embedded in algorithms, and what computing professionals need to consider when designing systems.

i_can_statements: I can break complex problems into smaller, manageable parts
I can identify patterns and use abstraction to generalize solutions
I can design algorithms to solve problems step-by-step
I can evaluate different approaches and select the best solution

competencies.raw: Design user interfaces and digital experiences
i_can_statements: I can design intuitive and user-friendly interfaces
I can consider accessibility and diverse user needs
I can prototype digital solutions and test them with users
I can iterate designs based on feedback
I can plan software projects with clear goals and requirements
I can design program architecture and structure
I can document designs and specifications
I can manage complexity in larger projects

competencies.raw: Design solutions that consider ethics and social impact
i_can_statements: I can consider privacy and security in software design
I can identify and mitigate bias in algorithms
I can design inclusive systems for diverse users
I can evaluate the social impact of computing solutions

competencies.raw: Write clean, functional code in multiple languages
i_can_statements: I can write code that is readable, logical, and well-documented
I can write programs in multiple programming languages
I can use proper syntax and follow style conventions
I can refactor code to improve quality and efficiency
I can identify and fix errors in code
I can use debugging tools and techniques
I can write tests to verify code functionality
I can handle errors gracefully

competencies.raw: Work with data structures and algorithms
i_can_statements: I can understand and implement common data structures
I can write efficient algorithms
I can analyze algorithm performance
I can select appropriate structures for different problems

competencies.raw: Develop and deliver complete software projects
i_can_statements: I can plan and execute multi-step coding projects
I can manage code versions and collaboration
I can document and present my work
I can deploy and maintain software
I can explain how computer hardware works
I can understand binary, memory, and storage
I can work with different operating systems
I can troubleshoot hardware and software issues

competencies.raw: Use software development tools and environments
i_can_statements: I can use integrated development environments (IDEs)
I can use version control systems
I can work with databases
I can use debugging and profiling tools
I can explain how networks and the internet work
I can understand basic cybersecurity principles
I can protect data and systems from threats
I can practice safe computing habits
I can understand artificial intelligence and machine learning basics
I can explore cloud computing and distributed systems
I can understand database design and management
I can learn about emerging technologies and trends

areas.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
topics: Algorithm efficiency and Big O notation

areas.name: Software Development and Advanced Topics
topics: Artificial intelligence and machine learning introductions
